what_it_does: End-to-end acceptance test for the RHM walkthrough/review organ. It drives the real Suite (no mocks) through a temporary FsStore/NodeRegistry, surfaces review items, runs a walkthrough session, exercises the ui:// component resolver, and asserts governance invariants: operator-only resolution, no status-lane writes to resolved, and refusal of contradictory terminal verdicts.
observations: Self-contained executable script using __main__ and sys.exit with a temporary store directory.
Seeds three generative review items via surface_review and asserts they start with resolved=None.
Verifies deterministic session lifecycle fields (cursor, item, total, done, opened steps) but treats LLM-derived framing as best-effort (None or str only).
Checks scheduler step firing by reading store.head for run://review-{sid}/step0 and step1.
respond() is tested to record a terminal verdict, stamp session_id and cursor position, and not advance the cursor.
Walks past the final item to verify done=True and idempotent next().
build_ui_info is checked for chrome regions (inbox/chat/activity), a canvas camera ref, and per-component capabilities.

Governance checks include status-lane set_status never writing resolved, resolve_surfaced writing resolved, second terminal resolve raising GovernanceError, and a source-code string check that resolve_surfaced is absent from the MCP face.
Uses global mutable counters PASS and ok in the check helper.
standouts: Drives the real Suite rather than mocks, making it an integration/acceptance test.
Verifies operator-only governance both by runtime refusal and by inspecting MCP source for the absence of resolve_surfaced calls.
Explicitly excludes non-deterministic LLM framing from hard assertions.
conventions: Executable test-as-script pattern with a main() and __main__ guard.
Isolated fixture via tempfile.mkdtemp and shutil.rmtree in a finally block.
Relative sys.path insertion to import from the project root.
Global mutable state (ok, PASS) for pass/fail bookkeeping.
Source-code string inspection to assert an API is not exposed on a face.
concerns: Global mutable PASS/ok counters make the test order-dependent and not thread-safe.
The MCP-face check is brittle: it looks for literal call substrings and can miss indirect calls or break with formatting changes.
Hard-coded internal address schemes (run://review-{sid}/step0, ui://review/{id}) couple the test to implementation details.
Importing mcp_server and reading its source inside main is late and assumes file layout/encoding.
No parameterized or structured test framework; a single failure stops counting but finally cleanup still runs.
notes: A runnable acceptance harness that turns the previously manual RHM walkthrough into codified, operator-paced governance and UI resolver checks.
questions: What are the exact implementations/contracts of surface_review, start_session, next, respond, resolve_surfaced, and build_ui_info?
How does the Suite compile the session into a Graph and schedule step firing?
What is the full set of walkthrough modes and how do they differ from other session modes?
Is the source-code absence check sufficient to guarantee no other MCP/agent entry point can call resolve_surfaced?
What fallback behavior is expected when the LLM providing framing is unavailable?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file performs the end-to-end lifecycle, ui:// resolver, and governance coverage described in the module docstring.
apparent_themes: Integration acceptance testing with real dependencies.
Deterministic assertions over non-deterministic LLM output.
UI component addressability via ui:// scheme.
Session lifecycle idempotence and operator pacing.
intention_signals: To automate and codify the previously manual/adversarial RHM walkthrough verification into a committed acceptance suite that proves the review organ is operator-paced and cannot be self-approved by code.
novelty: Distinct in combining runtime governance refusal tests with a source-code absence check for an API on the MCP face, and in explicitly carving out LLM-derived framing as best-effort rather than asserted.
contribution: A runnable end-to-end acceptance harness that documents and guards the RHM walkthrough session lifecycle, the ui:// resolver contract, and the operator-only resolution governance invariant.
summary_for_embedding: An executable acceptance test that drives the real Suite through a temporary store to verify the RHM walkthrough session lifecycle, the ui:// component resolver, and operator-only governance invariants.
intention_for_embedding: To provide committed automated coverage for the previously uncovered review/walkthrough organ and enforce that terminal verdicts remain operator-controlled and non-contradictable.

symbols.does: Evaluates a labeled boolean condition, updates global pass/fail counters, and prints PASS/FAIL.
symbols.description: The check helper is the file's assertion primitive. It takes a human-readable label and a condition, updates the global ok and PASS state, and emits a single-line result. Because it mutates globals, assertions are order-sensitive and the helper is used throughout main() for lifecycle, UI, and governance checks.

symbols.does: Sets up a real Suite in a temporary store and runs end-to-end walkthrough acceptance checks.
symbols.description: main() creates an isolated FsStore and NodeRegistry, instantiates Suite, seeds three review items, exercises start_session/present_current/next/respond/session_status, verifies the ui:// resolver via build_ui_info, and asserts governance invariants including operator-only resolve_surfaced, refusal of contradictory verdicts, and absence of resolve_surfaced on the MCP face. It returns 0 on full success and 1 on any failure, cleaning up the temp directory in a finally block.
